## Implementation Summary

| Item | Change | Files |
|------|--------|-------|
| Item 1 — Reflow menu not disabling | Changed `"enablement"` to `"when"` on the `reflowComment` submenu entry. VS Code ignores `enablement` in custom submenus; `when` correctly hides the item when `cursorInXmlComment` is false. | `package.json` |
| Item 2 — Filter icon wrong position | Moved `codicon-list-filter` span to immediately before the search input; transferred `margin-left:auto` from `.search-input` to the span; updated tooltip to `"Filter Anchors..."`. | `anchorsGridWebview.ts` |
| Item 3 — Overdue icon misaligned | Wrapped the warning icon span and text node in `<span style="display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;">`. Removed inline `marginRight` from icon. | `anchorsGridWebview.ts` |
| Item 4 — Colorization first-only | Replaced `commentPortion.toUpperCase().indexOf(tag)` (single call) with a `while` + `indexOf(tag, searchFrom)` loop. Now all occurrences of a tag on a line are decorated independently through the full word-boundary → colon → colorizeMode pipeline. | `anchorDecorationManager.ts` |

# Plan: Miscellaneous Bugs — Post-Implementation Follow-up

## Overview

Four bugs surfaced after the Plan 06 implementation. They fall across three areas: right-click menu command state, anchor grid webview UI, and inline tag colorization.

---

## Group A — Reflow Commands

### Item 1 — Reflow Current Comment Menu Not Disabling

**Request:** The "Reflow Current Comment" right-click menu entry should be disabled (grayed out or hidden) when the cursor is not inside an XML doc comment block.

**Current behavior:**
The submenu entry in `package.json` uses an `enablement` clause:

```json
// package.json:86-88
{
  "command": "kat-comment-studio.reflowComment",
  "group": "2_reflow@1",
  "enablement": "kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment"
}
```

VS Code's `enablement` property is intended to gray out a menu item when the condition is false. However, `enablement` is unreliable in nested submenu contributions — the item remains enabled regardless of cursor position.

**Root cause:**
VS Code does not consistently evaluate `enablement` for items inside custom submenus. The underlying context variable is set correctly (`extension.ts:276–307` sets `kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment` via `setContext` on every selection change and on activation), but the `enablement` expression is silently ignored in this context.

**Fix plan:**

Change `"enablement"` to `"when"` on the reflowComment submenu entry. This hides the item entirely when the cursor is outside an XML comment — cleaner UX than a grayed-out entry for a command that has no valid target.

```json
{
  "command": "kat-comment-studio.reflowComment",
  "group": "2_reflow@1",
  "when": "kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment"
}
```

**Files changed:** `package.json`

---

## Group B — Anchor Grid Webview

### Item 2 — Filter Icon Wrong Position in Toolbar

**Request:** The `codicon-list-filter` icon (the filter icon) should appear immediately to the left of the search/filter input, not immediately to the right of the scope dropdown. Also update its tooltip to `"Filter Anchors..."`.

**Current behavior:**
The toolbar HTML places the list-filter icon as the third child, right after the scope dropdown:

```html
<!-- anchorsGridWebview.ts (toolbar) -->
<div class="toolbar">
  <span class="codicon codicon-surround-with toolbar-icon" title="Scope"></span>
  <select class="scope-select" id="scopeSelect"></select>
  <span class="codicon codicon-list-filter toolbar-icon" title="Filter by type"></span>  <!-- ← here -->
  <input type="text" class="search-input" id="searchInput" placeholder="Filter anchors..." />
  <button class="filter-btn" id="filterBtn">Type ▾</button>
  <div class="filter-dropdown" id="filterDropdown"></div>
</div>
```

The `.search-input` CSS has `margin-left: auto`, which pulls the search input to the right edge. The list-filter icon is at position 3 in the flex row, so it visually lands next to the scope dropdown with a large gap before the search input:

```
[scope-icon] [scope-select] [list-filter] ··· gap ··· [search-input] [filter-btn]
```

**Root cause:**
`margin-left: auto` on a flex item absorbs all free space before *that item*. The list-filter span precedes the search input in DOM order, so it is placed before the auto-margin gap rather than inside it.

**Fix plan:**

1. Move the `<span codicon-list-filter>` in HTML to immediately before `<input searchInput>`.
2. Transfer `margin-left: auto` from `.search-input` CSS to the list-filter span (add it as an inline style or CSS rule), so the gap appears before the icon pair.
3. Change `title="Filter by type"` → `title="Filter Anchors..."`.

Desired visual result:
```
[scope-icon] [scope-select] ··· gap ··· [list-filter] [search-input] [filter-btn]
```

**Files changed:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

### Item 3 — Overdue Warning Icon Vertical Misalignment

**Request:** The warning icon and date text in the overdue due-date cell should be vertically centered. Currently there is a noticeable gap (misalignment) between the icon and the text.

**Current behavior:**
The cell is built by appending a codicon span followed by a raw text node:

```typescript
// anchorsGridWebview.ts:793-799
const icon = document.createElement('span');
icon.className = 'codicon codicon-warning';
icon.style.marginRight = '4px';
dueDateCell.appendChild(icon);
dueDateCell.appendChild(document.createTextNode(dueDateDisplay));
```

The `td` contains an `inline-block` codicon span (rendered at `font-size: 16px` with its own line-height) alongside a text node (at the cell's font size). Inline-block elements and adjacent text nodes are baseline-aligned by default, but the codicon's cap height and descent metrics differ from the text's, producing a visible vertical shift.

**Root cause:**
No explicit vertical alignment is enforced. The two inline fragments share a baseline but not a midline, so the icon appears offset relative to the text.

**Fix plan:**

Wrap the icon and text node in a `<span>` with `display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:4px`. Remove the inline `marginRight` from the icon (gap handles spacing). The flex container forces both items to share the same vertical midpoint regardless of font metrics.

```typescript
const wrapper = document.createElement('span');
wrapper.style.cssText = 'display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;';
const icon = document.createElement('span');
icon.className = 'codicon codicon-warning';
wrapper.appendChild(icon);
wrapper.appendChild(document.createTextNode(dueDateDisplay));
dueDateCell.appendChild(wrapper);
```

**Files changed:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

## Group C — Anchor Inline Colorization

### Item 4 — Colorization Only Decorates First Tag Occurrence Per Line

**Request:** All occurrences of a registered tag keyword on a line should be colorized, not just the first one. With `caseSensitive` mode, a bare uppercase `BUG` should colorize and a bare lowercase `note` should not.

**Current behavior:**
Given `// BUG: This is BUG and note hello`:

- With `caseSensitive`: neither the standalone `BUG` nor `note` colorizes.
- With `caseInsensitive`: only `note` colorizes (standalone `BUG` is missed).

Expected with `caseSensitive`: standalone `BUG` (uppercase, exact match) colorizes; `note` (lowercase, does not match `NOTE`) does not.

**Root cause:**
`updateDecorations()` uses `String.prototype.indexOf(tag)` with no start offset — a single call that finds only the **first** occurrence of the tag on a line:

```typescript
// anchorDecorationManager.ts:119
const tagIdx = commentPortion.toUpperCase().indexOf(tag);
if (tagIdx < 0) continue;
// ... processes first match only, then exits the entry loop
```

For `// BUG: This is BUG and note hello`, the first occurrence is `BUG:` (at position 3). The code processes it (hasColon=true → always colorizes), then moves on to the next decoration type. The second bare `BUG` at ~position 16 is never visited.

**Fix plan:**

Replace the single `indexOf` call with a `while` loop that advances `searchFrom` past each match, processing every occurrence through the full pipeline (word-boundary check → colon check → colorizeMode filter):

```typescript
let searchFrom = 0;
while (true) {
  const tagIdx = commentPortion.toUpperCase().indexOf(tag, searchFrom);
  if (tagIdx < 0) break;
  searchFrom = tagIdx + 1; // advance past this match for next iteration

  const absIdx = commentStart + tagIdx;
  // ... existing word-boundary, colon, colorizeMode logic unchanged ...
}
```

After this fix:
- `caseSensitive`: `BUG:` colorizes (hasColon), standalone `BUG` colorizes (`'BUG' === 'BUG'`), `note` does not (`'note' !== 'NOTE'`). ✓
- `caseInsensitive`: all three colorize. ✓

**Files changed:** `src/anchors/anchorDecorationManager.ts`
